steps.title: Use the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content: The triangle is right-angled at $B$, so $AC$ is the hypotenuse. Use $AC^2=AB^2+BC^2$ with the two given leg lengths.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Since angle $B$ is the right angle, $AC$ is the hypotenuse. Apply the Pythagorean theorem:
$$AC^2 = (7\sqrt{41})^2 + (24\sqrt{41})^2$$
Expand each term:
$$AC^2 = 49 \cdot 41 + 576 \cdot 41$$
Factor out 41:
$$AC^2 = 41(49 + 576) = 41 \cdot 625$$

steps.title: Recognize the perfect square
steps.content:
Observe that $49 + 576 = 625 = 25^2$ (note: $7^2 + 24^2 = 25^2$, a Pythagorean triple). Therefore:
$$AC^2 = 41 \cdot 25^2 = (25\sqrt{41})^2$$
Taking the positive square root:
$$AC = 25\sqrt{41}$$
B is correct.
